calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiff First Cause Of Action
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joe Lake, Esq., CCSF Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.4217, for defendants/moving party;
Craig Martin, Esq., T:415.640.4803, for plaintiff/ opposing party.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel submits to the tentative ruling. There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS MASOOD ORDIKHANI, HARLAN KELLY, IVY FINE, BENJAMIN POOLE, MICHAL BROWN, SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, KATHY HOW's Motion To Strike Plaintiff First Cause Of Action as follows:

Defendants' motion to strike Plaintiff's first cause of action and claim for punitive damages is granted as unopposed. In his late-filed opposition, Plaintiff Craig K. Martin, Sr. concedes that "individuals cannot be held liable for discrimination," and does not contest Defendants' showing that the Government Claims Act forbids punitive damages as a remedy against public entities. Accordingly, the claims against all individual Defendants are stricken, as are the claims for punitive damages.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joe Lake, Esq., CCSF Deputy City Attorney, T:415.554.4217, for defendants/moving party;
Craig Martin, Esq., T:415.640.4803, for plaintiff/ opposing party.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel submits to the tentative ruling. There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS MASOOD ORDIKHANI, HARLAN KELLY, IVY FINE, BENJAMIN POOLE, MICHAL BROWN, SAN FRANCISCO PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, KATHY HOW's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's Verified First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action other than the first and third causes of action against the City, as to which the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend.

In his late-filed 4-page opposition, Plaintiff Craig K. Martin, Sr. does not respond meaningfully, or at all, to Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action for negligent hiring, training, retention or supervision; the fourth cause of action for age discrimination; the fifth cause of action for libel per se; the sixth and seventh causes of action for intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress; or the eighth and ninth causes of action for interference with prospective economic advantage. To the extent that the court can discern Plaintiff's position, he seeks to pursue only his claims for retaliation, harassment, and racial discrimination, which are included in the first cause of action, to which the City has not demurred, and the third cause of action. Those causes of action are defective because, among other things, Plaintiff does not explicitly allege that the allegedly harassing behavior by defendants Ordikhani or Poole was due to his race. Nor does he clearly allege what protected activity allegedly prompted the claimed retaliation. Plaintiff is granted 20 days leave to plead cognizable claims for relief, which shall be separately stated in accordance with Cal. Rules of Court, rule 2.112. If Plaintiff fails timely to file an amended complaint, the action will be dismissed. (Code Civ. Proc., 581(f)(2).)

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
